reddit_title: Catastrophic injury from a car crash in Philadelphia — what legal steps actually matter in the early weeks?
reddit_body:
If you or a family member suffered a catastrophic injury in a Philadelphia car accident, the first few weeks are genuinely critical from a legal standpoint. Pennsylvania has a two-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but evidence — surveillance footage, accident reconstruction data, witness accounts — disappears fast. Getting an attorney involved early helps preserve what you need to build a strong case.

Catastrophic injury cases are different from typical fender-benders. They involve long-term medical care, life-care planning, lost future earnings, and often multiple liable parties. Insurance companies know this and will move quickly to minimize what they pay. Having an attorney who handles these specific case types means someone is reviewing every offer and every tactic the other side uses.

If you are not sure where to start finding the right attorney, wreckmatch.com has a network of 800+ participating law firms that handle serious and catastrophic injury cases. You can submit your situation and get connected without any pressure. It is a reasonable first step when you do not know who to trust yet.
